items.headline: Avalanche Warning issued April 24 at 7:51AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
SABAFC

The following message is transmitted at the request of the
Valdez Avalanche Center.

...THE VALDEZ AVALANCHE CENTER HAS ISSUED AN AVALANCHE WARNING...

* WHAT... Wet heavy snow and strong southeasterly winds are
overloading a weak snowpack. Very dangerous avalanche conditions
exist.

* WHERE...For the mountains surrounding the Valdez and Thompson
Pass area, including Ernestine.

* WHEN...In effect from 8AM FRIDAY (TODAY) THROUGH 8AM SATURDAY
AKST.

* IMPACTS...Continued heavy snowfall, w

items.headline: High Wind Watch issued April 24 at 12:55PM UTC until April 27 at 12:00AM UTC by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South to southwest winds 35 to 55 mph, with gusts up to 70
to 80 mph possible.

* WHERE...Nikolski and Unalaska.

* WHEN...From Saturday evening through Sunday afternoon.

* IMPACTS...High winds could move loose debris, damage property, and
cause power outages. Travel could be difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...The highest forecast wind speed will depend
on the track of a low in the Bering Sea, which is uncertain at
this time. If the low tracks further east, gusts as high as 70-80
mph a
items.instruction:
A high wind watch means there is potential for a hazardous high wind
event. Monitor the latest forecasts and warnings for updates on this
situation. Fasten loose objects or shelter objects in a safe
location prior to the onset of winds.

items.headline: High Wind Watch issued April 24 at 12:55PM UTC until April 27 at 12:00AM UTC by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Southwest winds 45 to 65 mph, with gusts up to 70 to 80 mph
possible.

* WHERE...Adak and Atka.

* WHEN...From Saturday afternoon through Sunday afternoon.

* IMPACTS...High winds could move loose debris, damage property, and
cause power outages. Travel could be difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strong easterly winds move in Saturday
morning, but the highest winds and worst conditions will begin
when winds turn southerly Saturday afternoon. There may be a
period of weaker winds Saturda
items.instruction:
A high wind watch means there is potential for a hazardous high wind
event. Monitor the latest forecasts and warnings for updates on this
situation. Fasten loose objects or shelter objects in a safe
location prior to the onset of winds.

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 23 at 12:27PM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 35 to 45 mph with gusts up to 65 mph expected.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: High Wind Warning issued April 23 at 12:27PM AKDT until April 24 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 40 to 50 mph with gusts up to 75 mph.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...Until 4 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 23 at 12:27PM AKDT until April 25 at 4:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Southeast winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts up to 50 mph
expected.

* WHERE...Delta Junction.

* WHEN...From 4 AM Friday to 4 AM AKDT Saturday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...There may be a brief decrease in wind speeds
Friday afternoon.
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.
